LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH, AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFFS' SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, VIOLATION OF THE CLRA. PLAINTIFFS MUST PLEAD REPRESENTATIONS BEYOND MERE "PUFFERY" OR THAT RELATE DIRECTLY TO THE ALLEGED DEFECT. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, VIOLATION OF THE UCL, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE WITH PARTICULARITY THE FRAUDULENT BUSINESS PRACTICE OR THE VIOLATION OF LAW. THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUDULENT CONCEALMENT/NONDISCLOSURE LACKS SPECIFICITY. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT ADEQUATELY ALLEGE A SERIOUS PERSONAL SAFETY RISK THAT PLAINTIFFS, OR ANY IDENTIFIABLE CLASS MEMBER, EXPERIENCED. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF EXPRESS WARRANTY, NO CLAIM IS STATED BECAUSE LATENT DEFECTS THAT ARE DISCOVERED AFTER EXPRESS WARRANTY EXPIRATION ARE NOT COVERED. DOUGHERTY VS. AMERICAN HONDA (2006) 144 CAL.APP. 4TH 824. THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR UNJUST ENRICHMENT IS DEPENDENT UPON THE SURVIVAL OF ANOTHER CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT WOULD NOT FIND ANY OF THESE CLAIMS TO BE TIME-BARRED FOR PURPOSES OF DEMURRER. (DEFENSE COUNSEL TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST, SECOND, THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFFS' THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECTS CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. CLAIMED INJURY OCCURRED OUTSIDE OF WARRANTY PERIOD. PLAINTIFFS DO NOT ADEQUATELY ALLEGE A SERIOUS PERSONAL SAFETY RISK TO ANY OF THE NAMED PLAINTIFFS OR IDENTIFIABLE CLASS MEMBERS. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO ESTABLISH A DUTY TO DISCLOSE THE ALLEGED DEFECT, RELIANCE, OR A VIOLATION OF LAW. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT IDENTIFY ANY MISREPRESENTATIONS MADE ABOUT THE POWER VALVE SCREWS, OR AN OMMISSION CONTRARY TO AN ACTUAL REPRESENTATION MADE BY DEFENDANT. NOTHING IN THE CITED ADVERTISING CAMPAIGN RELATES DIRECTLY TO THE ALLEGED DEFECT, AND THE REPRESENTATIONS CONSTITUTE MERE "PUFFERY." FAILURE TO DISCLOSE A DEFECT THAT, MIGHT OR MIGHT NOT, SHORTEN THE EFFECTIVE LIFE SPAN OF AN AUTOMOBILIE/PART THAT FUNCTIONS PRECISELY AS WARRANTED THROUGHOUT THE TERM OF ITS EXPRESS WARRANTY CANNOT BE CHARACTERIZED AS CAUSING A SUBSTANTIAL INJURY TO CONSUMERS. SEE DAUGHERTY V. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC. (2006) 144 CAL.APP.4TH 824. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH THIRTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING AN AGREEMENT AND TRANSACTION THAT OCCURRED BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH THIRTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD TO PLEAD WITH SPECIFICITY, PLEAD RELIANCE AND INTENT TO DECEIVE. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH THIRTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF EXPRESS WARRANTY AND FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF IMPLIED WARRANTY. LATENT DEFECTS THAT ARE DISCOVERED AFTER EXPIRATION OF THE EXPRESS WARRANTY ARE NOT COVERED. (DAUGHERTY VS. AMERICAN HONDA (2006) 144 CAL.APP. 4TH 824.) NO VERTICAL PRIVITY TO SUPPORT IMPLIED WARRANTY, OR ALLEGATION PATHFINDER WAS PURCHASED NEW. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR B&P CODE SECTION 17200 CLAIMS AND SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR UNJUST ENRICHMENT. THE COURT ADDS: ALL RULINGS ARE MADE WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RAISING ANY OF THESE ISSUES AGAIN. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Pltff'S Second Set Of Special Interrogatories, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LELAND H. BELEW & ATTY. LORIS E. ANDRUS OF THE ANDRUS ANDERSON LLP, TEL. NO. 986-1400 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. PAUL J. RIEHLE OF THE SEDWICK LLP, TEL. NO. 781-7900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS ADRIAN FOX, WILLIAM MCMULLEN, JR. AND SCOTT WINKLER'S Motion to Compel Further Responses To Pltff'S Second Set Of Special Interrogatories, And For Sanctions - Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to Special Interrogatories 1 and 2 is granted in part and denied in part. The Court grants Class Action Plaintiffs' discovery of the names and addresses and Vin Numbers of putative class members. The Court has weighed balancing the need for discovery with constitutionally protected privacy rights and imposes appropriate third-party privacy protections. (See Pioneer Electronics (USA), Inc. v. Super. Ct. (2007) 40 Cal.4th 360, 373; Hill v. Nat'l Collegiate Athletic Assn. (1994) 7Cal. 4th 1.) The privacy safeguard shall be a protective order limiting viewing of the names and addresses to attorney-eyes only. That portion of the Plaintiffs' motion seeking discovery of putative class members' email addresses and/or phone numbers is denied. The scope of the names and addresses of car owners who have not made a claim under warranty is "owners of subject vehicles equipped with power valve engine screws in the intake manifold." Plaintiffs' attempt to expand the definition to include screws "that are not locked tight or may be prone to loosening" is denied without prejudice. Plaintiffs failed to offer proof in their moving papers showing that Defendant is aware of which vehicles meet that added definition or that Defendant has that information. Plaintiffs' supplemental filing also does not meet the necessary level of proof. Defendant's objection that the interrogatory is overbroad is sustained to the extent it seeks to include the expanded definition. Special Interrogatory 2 seeking to identify individuals who have filed a complaint shall be answered separately. The Defendant's objection that the information being sought was previously a topic of discovery is overruled. The parties shall "meet and confer" to work out the language of an appropriate protective order regarding the information to be provided in response to Special Interrogatories 1 and 2. Plaintiffs' request for monetary sanctions are denied. (See Order for further details) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-05-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST, SECOND, THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFFS' THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Michael F. Ram, Esq. of Ram & Olson LLP 433-4949 & Jennie Lee Anderson, Esq. of Andrus Anderson LLP 986-1400 for pltfs & opposing parties; Paul J. Riehle, Esq. of Sedgwick, Detert, Moran & Arnold LLP 627-1554 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECTS CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. CLAIMED INJURY OCCURRED OUTSIDE OF WARRANTY PERIOD. PLAINTIFFS DO NOT ADEQUATELY ALLEGE A SERIOUS PERSONAL SAFETY RISK TO ANY OF THE NAMED PLAINTIFFS OR IDENTIFIABLE CLASS MEMBERS. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO ESTABLISH A DUTY TO DISCLOSE THE ALLEGED DEFECT, RELIANCE, OR A VIOLATION OF LAW. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT IDENTIFY ANY MISREPRESENTATIONS MADE ABOUT THE POWER VALVE SCREWS, OR AN OMMISSION CONTRARY TO AN ACTUAL REPRESENTATION MADE BY DEFENDANT. NOTHING IN THE CITED ADVERTISING CAMPAIGN RELATES DIRECTLY TO THE ALLEGED DEFECT, AND THE REPRESENTATIONS CONSTITUTE MERE "PUFFERY." FAILURE TO DISCLOSE A DEFECT THAT, MIGHT OR MIGHT NOT, SHORTEN THE EFFECTIVE LIFE SPAN OF AN AUTOMOBILIE/PART THAT FUNCTIONS PRECISELY AS WARRANTED THROUGHOUT THE TERM OF ITS EXPRESS WARRANTY CANNOT BE CHARACTERIZED AS CAUSING A SUBSTANTIAL INJURY TO CONSUMERS. SEE DAUGHERTY V. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC. (2006) 144 CAL.APP.4TH 824. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice And Demurrer To First, Second, Third, Fourth, And Fifth Causes Of Action In Pltfs' Second Amended Complaint; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Apr-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC. Demurrer to First, Second, Third, Fourth, and Fifth Causes of Action in Plaintiffs' Second Amended Complaint. Appearances: Jennie Lee Anderson, Esq. of Andrus Anderson LLP (415) 986-1440 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Paul J. Riehle, Esq. of Sedgwick Detert, Oran & Arnold LLP (415) 781-7900, appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with 30 days leave to amend. As to the first cause of action, violation of the CLRA. Plaintiffs must plead representations beyond mere "puffery" or that relate directly to the alleged defect. As to the second cause of action, violation of the UCL, plaintiff must allege with particularity the fraudulent business practice or the violation of law. The third cause of action for fraudulent concealment/nondisclosure lacks specificity. The complaint does not adequately allege a serious personal safety risk that plaintiffs, or any identifiable class member, experienced. As to the fourth cause of action for breach of express warranty, no claim is stated because latent defects that are discovered after express warranty expiration are not covered. Dougherty vs. American Honda (2006) 144 Cal.App. 4th 824. The fifth cause of action for unjust enrichment is dependent upon the survival of another cause of action. The Court would not find any of these claims to be time-barred for purposes of demurrer. (Defense Counsel to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Jennie Anderson, Esq. & Lori E. Andrus, Esq. of Andrus Anderson LLP (415) 986-1440 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Paul J. Riehle, Esq. of Sedwick, Detert, (415) 781-7900 appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer sustained with thirty days leave to amend as to first cause of action. Plaintiff must allege facts supporting an agreement and transaction that occurred between plaintiff and defendant. Demurrer sustained with thirty days leave to amend as to the third cause of action for fraud to plead with specificity, plead reliance and intent to deceive. Demurrer sustained with thirty days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for breach of express warranty and fifth cause of action for breach of implied warranty. Latent defects that are discovered after expiration of the express warranty are not covered. (Daugherty vs. American Honda (2006) 144 Cal.App. 4th 824.) No vertical privity to support implied warranty, or allegation Pathfinder was purchased new. Demurrer overruled as to second cause of action for B&P Code section 17200 claims and sixth cause of action for unjust enrichment. The Court adds: All rulings are made without prejudice to raising any of these issues again. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
